The Integration Pitch

Why BridgeGate, why now

Weiser Memorial Hospital is running Likely TruBridge/Evident, Change Healthcare confirmed as the billing/claims vendor side by side. Every one of those speaks a different dialect of HL7/FHIR/X12 — which means someone on staff is today doing by hand (or not doing at all) what BridgeGate does automatically: keeping one patient's record consistent everywhere it needs to show up. A September 2024 cybersecurity incident took the hospital's computer systems down for weeks with intermittent phone outages — direct evidence of IT fragility, though which systems/vendors were affected wasn't disclosed. MAJOR: a separate Sept 2024 Embargo ransomware attack caused weeks of system/phone outages, ~200GB of stolen data, and a disclosed breach of 59,990 patients' SSNs/medical/insurance information (public notice May 2025) — combined with the Change Healthcare disruption, the strongest cybersecurity/vendor-churn signal found across all 44 hospitals.

With a lean IT team stretched thin, the pitch isn't 'do more work' — it's 'let BridgeGate and AI carry the integration load so your team doesn't have to.'

The payoff is concrete: one integration layer retires the redundant interfaces and manual data movement that quietly drain the budget, streamlining operations and taking roughly 35% out of what a hospital your size spends on IT.

Manish
